signals.headline: SEC Lawsuit Against Ripple Fully Resolved, Triggering XRP Surge to $1
signals.raw:
SEC Lawsuit Against Ripple Fully Resolved, Triggering XRP Surge to $1.50+ | YES | HIGH confidence**  
The SEC has dropped its long-running lawsuit against Ripple Labs, removing a major regulatory overhang and driving a 10% XRP price surge in April 2026, with the token breaking above $1.50 before consolidating at ~$1.42.[1] This resolution, confirmed as final, has shifted market sentiment toward in

signals.raw:
Inflation Rose 3.3% Over Past 12 Months; Fed Expected to Hold Rates at April 28 Meeting** | YES | HIGH confidence[2]

signals.headline: Elon Musk Tweet Count Markets Trending on Polymarket | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Elon Musk Tweet Count Markets Trending on Polymarket | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Polymarket's most active markets focus on **Elon Musk's tweet volumes**, such as "Elon Musk # tweets April 17 - April 24, 2026?" (29% odds for 180-199 range) and "Elon Musk # tweets in April 2026?" (18-28% odds for 1120-1159 range), with over $19-21M in trading volume across 130+ related markets.[2][3][4]

signals.headline: High Tweet Volume Bets Driving Activity | YES | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
High Tweet Volume Bets Driving Activity | YES | MED confidence**  
Traders are heavily engaged in ranges like **1120-1159 tweets** (18-28% implied probability), reflecting real-time sentiment shifts on Musk's posting habits amid pop culture buzz, with prices updating continuously on new data.[2][3][4] No specific whale bets or X posts are detailed, but massive volumes ($3M+ overall) suggest smart

signals.headline: Trending Tweet Markets Dominate Over Others | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Trending Tweet Markets Dominate Over Others | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Beyond Musk, **Kanye tweet markets** like "Will Kanye tweet again by April 30?" remain active (131 markets total), but Musk-focused ones lead with top odds and volume; broader "Trending Markets" (166 live) include niche events like Nasdaq trading changes, lacking specific X chatter or moves.[1][3]
